title: Organize CSS into Layers
description: Take flat CSS and organize it into @layer order.
hint: Separate into settings, generic, elements, objects, components, utilities.
solution: @layer settings, generic, elements, objects, components, utilities; @layer settings { :root { --primary: blue; } }

title: Extract Objects from Components
description: Identify layout patterns in component CSS and extract them as reusable objects.
hint: Look for repeated grid/flex patterns in components. Extract to .o-grid class.
solution: .o-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(250px, 1fr)); gap: 1rem; } Then use on component containers.

title: Audit and Fix Architecture
description: Given a project with no architecture, design and implement one.
hint: Analyze current CSS, create layer plan, restructure files, implement @layer.
solution: 1) Audit 2) Create architecture doc 3) Set up file structure 4) Implement @layer 5) Move selectors to correct layers.

title: Implement ITCSS File Structure
description: Create the full ITCSS file structure with proper @import order.
hint: Create directories and files for each ITCSS layer, import in order.
solution: settings/_variables.css → tools/_mixins.css → generic/_reset.css → elements/_typography.css → objects/_grid.css → components/_card.css → utilities/_spacing.css → main.css (imports all)

title: Design Architecture for a Design System
description: Design a complete CSS architecture for a large design system supporting multiple brands.
hint: Consider: brand tokens, component API, theme switching, cascade layers, file organization.
solution: Design system layers: @layer design-tokens, reset, base, objects, components, patterns, overrides. Each brand provides its settings layer.
